criteria.name: Problem Identification
criteria.description: Ability to clearly identify the statistical problem or question from the given ill-defined scenario.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Accurately identifies the null and alternative hypotheses, variables involved, and the type of test required.
criteria.poor_indicator: Fails to distinguish between null and alternative hypotheses or misidentifies the variables and test type.

criteria.name: Assumptions Recognition
criteria.description: Understanding and listing the assumptions related to the statistical test being considered.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Correctly identifies all necessary assumptions for the chosen hypothesis test (e.g., normality, independence, sample size).
criteria.poor_indicator: Overlooks key assumptions or lists irrelevant assumptions for the test.

criteria.name: Data Requirements
criteria.description: Ability to specify the type and amount of data needed for the hypothesis test.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Clearly defines the data type (e.g., continuous, categorical) and the sample size needed for the test.
criteria.poor_indicator: Provides vague or incorrect data requirements, or fails to mention sample size.

typical_errors: Failure to specify sample size or data type

criteria.name: Error Identification in AI Output
criteria.description: Ability to identify errors or gaps in the AI-generated hypothesis testing output.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Correctly identifies all statistical errors and logical inconsistencies in the AI output.
criteria.poor_indicator: Misses key errors or incorrectly identifies correct information as erroneous.

criteria.name: Statistical Validity Assessment
criteria.description: Judging the validity of the statistical methods and results presented by the AI.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Accurately evaluates the appropriateness of statistical tests and the correctness of results.
criteria.poor_indicator: Accepts incorrect statistical methods or results without question.

criteria.name: Assumption Verification
criteria.description: Checking if the AI output adheres to the statistical assumptions outlined in the framing phase.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Thoroughly verifies that all necessary assumptions are met or appropriately addressed.
criteria.poor_indicator: Fails to check for assumption adherence or overlooks violations.

typical_errors: Missing logical inconsistencies in hypothesis tests

criteria.name: Feedback Clarity
criteria.description: Providing clear and precise feedback to the AI to correct errors or improve output.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Feedback is specific, actionable, and directly addresses identified errors.
criteria.poor_indicator: Feedback is vague, overly general, or fails to address the specific issues.

criteria.name: Statistical Guidance
criteria.description: Ability to guide the AI towards using appropriate statistical methods and interpretations.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Suggests correct statistical procedures and interpretations to improve AI output.
criteria.poor_indicator: Provides incorrect or irrelevant statistical guidance.

criteria.name: Iterative Improvement
criteria.description: Effectively steering the AI through iterative cycles to refine the hypothesis testing output.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Demonstrates a clear strategy for iterative improvement and successfully refines the output.
criteria.poor_indicator: Fails to improve the output across iterations or repeats the same feedback without progress.

typical_errors: Providing feedback that is too vague to implement
